summary: Draft PR closed without merge that added missing library decomp work for `lb_80019628`, `lbMemory_80015320`, and `lbMemory_8001564C`, plus a small `fn_80015184` matching improvement. The bot reported `fn_80015184` reaching 100%, large partial improvements for the new functions, but also broken matches/regressions in nearby `lbmemory` items, so this is useful as a reference attempt rather than an accepted final solution.

key_files.role: Added `lb_80019628`, which updates timing state in `lb_804329F0`, derives a PAD sampling rate, and manages an `OSSetPeriodicAlarm` callback to `lb_80019900`.

Added AR/devcom includes, implemented `lbMemory_80015320` and `lbMemory_8001564C`, moved `LBMgr`/`lbl_803BA2C0` earlier, and changed `fn_80015184` to re-arm with `OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3)` and `fn_80015184`.

what_changed: Implemented `lb_80019628(void)` after `lb_80019AAC`, using `lb_804329F0` timing fields, `OS_TIMER_CLOCK`, `PADSetSamplingRate`, `OSCancelAlarm`, `OSCreateAlarm`, and `OSSetPeriodicAlarm`.

Promoted `lbMemory_80015320` from a static forward declaration to a real function and public header declaration. The implementation walks a `Handle` chain, updates `g_alloc.x6E4`, uses `HSD_DevComRequest` for addresses below `0x80000000`, otherwise populates an `LBMgr` at `&g_alloc + 0x6A0` and schedules `fn_80015184` via `OSSetAlarm`.

Implemented `lbMemory_8001564C(void)` to initialize AR-backed allocator state: calls `ARAlloc`, `ARFree`, and `ARGetSize`, caps arena high at `0x01000000`, builds free handle lists in `g_alloc.x8` and `g_alloc.x638`, and seeds `g_alloc.x69C` with `lbMemory_80014E24`.
Changed `fn_80015184`'s re-arm call from raw `0x10624DD3, NULL` to `OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3), fn_80015184`; the bot reported this item improved from 88.61% to 100.00%.
Added required includes for `<dolphin/ar.h>` and `<baselib/devcom.h>`, and moved `struct LBMgr` plus the `"lbmemory.c"` static string earlier so the new function can reference them.
smart_moves: Kept the allocator layout guarded by `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(g_alloc) == 0x6F0)` while adding code that depends on raw offsets such as `0x6A0`.
Used semantic SDK macros/APIs like `OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3)` instead of a magic tick constant, which coincided with `fn_80015184` becoming a new full match in the bot report.
Modeled the asynchronous transfer path with both `HSD_DevComRequest` and an `OSAlarm`-driven fallback, preserving callback-style recursion through `lbMemory_80015320`.
Bracketed `LBMgr` state writes with `OSDisableInterrupts`/`OSRestoreInterrupts`, matching the kind of critical-section behavior expected around shared alarm/copy state.
Added only the necessary header declaration for `lbMemory_80015320`, keeping the address-comment declaration style used by existing `lbmemory.h` entries.
decomp_lessons: A semantic replacement for a magic timer constant can be a matching tactic when it reproduces the original value and callback behavior; here `OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3)` plus the handler argument was enough for a reported full match of `fn_80015184`.

Adding missing functions can disturb already-matched neighbors through data ordering or codegen. The bot reported broken matches for `lbMemory_80014E24` and `lbMemory_800154D4`, plus `.data` and `lbMemory_80014FC8` regressions, so future reuse should verify those before transplanting this code.

For allocator-style decomp, preserve handle-list pointer chains and raw field offsets until structs are better understood; this PR used `x0_next`, `x4_lo`, `x8_hi`, `x62C_free_mem`, and `x698_free_heap` consistently.
When a function is used as an async callback, its signature may need apparently unused parameters and explicit casts; avoid simplifying those too early if matching is the goal.
Because this PR was closed unmerged and had no human review comments, treat the implementations as partial evidence, especially `lb_80019628`, which the bot reported at only 50.52%.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions retained address-based names: `lb_80019628`, `lbMemory_80015320`, and `lbMemory_8001564C`.
Module prefix casing was preserved: lowercase `lb_` for `lb_0192.c` and camel-cased `lbMemory_` for memory-manager functions.
Struct fields used offset-style names where semantics were still incomplete, such as `x0_arenaLo`, `x4_arenaHi`, `x62C_free_mem`, `x6E4`, `x6E8`, `x0_next`, `x4_lo`, and `x8_hi`.
Known static labels remained address-style, e.g. `lbl_803BA2C0` for the `"lbmemory.c"` string and comment `/// lbMemory_804318B0` above `g_alloc`.
Header declarations followed the existing address-comment style, e.g. `/* 015320 */ void lbMemory_80015320(...)`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Rounded transfer sizes with `((size + 0x1F) & ~0x1F)`, matching 0x20-byte alignment behavior before devcom/alarm copy scheduling.
Used explicit `(u32)` and pointer casts around allocator addresses and sizes to steer pointer arithmetic/codegen.
Kept assertion strings as offsets from `"lbmemory.c"` via `temp_r3 + 0xB4` and `temp_r3 + 0xA8`, a common tactic when original string data is packed near the file-name string.
Used an empty `if (ARGetSize() > 0x01000000U) { } else { ... }` branch shape while capping `arenaHi`, suggesting control-flow preservation for matching.
Changed the alarm re-schedule in `fn_80015184` to `OSSetAlarm(&temp_r3->alarm, OS_MSEC_TO_TICKS(3), fn_80015184)`, which the bot identified as the one new full match.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 66.98% with -128 bytes overall for GALE01.
Bot-positive result: 1 new match, `main/melee/lb/lbmemory` item `fn_80015184`, improved from 88.61% to 100.00%.
Bot partial improvements: `lbMemory_8001564C` from 0.00% to 93.52%, `lbMemory_80015320` from 0.00% to 91.00%, and `lb_80019628` from 0.00% to 50.52%.
Bot regressions: broken matches in `lbMemory_80014E24` and `lbMemory_800154D4`, plus regressions in `.data` and `lbMemory_80014FC8`.
PR metadata shows it was a draft, closed unmerged, with `mergeable_state` recorded as `dirty`; the slice does not explain the closure reason.
follow_up_queries: Find later merged PRs touching `lbMemory_80015320` or `lbMemory_8001564C` to identify the accepted final implementation.
Compare later `lb_80019628` work because this PR's version only reached 50.52% according to the bot.
Investigate why adding these functions broke `lbMemory_80014E24` and `lbMemory_800154D4` before reusing the data/struct ordering.
Check whether `lbMemory_80015320` should remain public in `lbmemory.h` or be limited to callback/internal visibility in the accepted code.
Audit final names and types for `lb_804329F0` fields used by `lb_80019628`, since this PR still used raw `x` fields.
